title: Constraint-guided bootstrap for a single-channel observable
research_question: What is the strongest model-independent bound on a single-channel observable using positivity / analyticity / dispersive constraints under a laptop-feasible setup?
strategy_notes: Start from a single-channel representation and implement typed positivity constraints on spectral densities or moments. Treat normalization choices as explicit assumptions with auditable priors and constraint binding.

evaluation_metrics.description: Quantify tightening versus a published or synthetic baseline at fixed kinematic points.
evaluation_metrics.target: At least one numerically reproducible bound stronger or qualitatively different than the baseline.

next_actions: Extract normalizations/definitions and the exact positivity inequality assumptions from seed papers (LaTeX-first).
Specify an explicit optimization variable set and constraint set (laptop-feasible).
Add a numerics validation plan (toy checks + regression outputs).

key_artifacts.description: Per-island plan (this artifact) used by validate-project to enforce required structure.
